play_store_reviews.text: There are several features that don't work from a phone: When editing a comment post, in the messages section of a group, the attachment (paper clip) icon and other formatting options are not available. So, if you forget to add a pic, then you have to re-post or get on a computer. Saving drafts of posts is also a problem. Ping functions are no where to be found in the phone app for Android. There are a few other difficulties but I can't remember them at the moment.

The company I work for uses Basecamp countless times on a daily basis. On desktop computers. The app...is another story. I have never had full functionality of the app on my phone. 95% of the time no campfire conversations, pings, to-do's etc etc ever loaded, regardless of how long I waited. The other 5% of the time it functioned after restarting the app multiple times. Since the most recent couple of updates, it now doesn't load 100% of the time.

Plenty of glitches where the app is inaccessible for days at a time. Not a very logical way to organize bookmarks, and the search function is not efficient, nor does it seem to follow the established protocol for search terms. Overall disorganized, inefficient and hard to navigate. I have to use it since it's the only hub for a curriculum my family uses. Unfortunate.

It has its perks sure but it's not like I can use it because it is Always offline!! I can't respond to any of my important messages (and it has nothing to do with my internet connection) and have to get on the desktop app to do Anything, which defeats the purpose of having a mobile app. Sometimes restarting it helps but usually I just have to get on a PC. I really wish desktop app had audio cues for notifications.

Notifications are turned on. All my other apps notify me even ones I don't need. This one, I need, I've allowed, I've cleared cache, uninstalled reinstalled, turned my phone off and back on again. Tried the reset my phone and even checked for updates on both phone and app. Notifications show up in my email tho. I used to get Notifications in both my email and from the app on my phone. The app is faster sending them to my phone yet the emails are the only ones showing up all the sudden.

This app is great. I had always been averse to trying Basecamp because of the cost, even though a lot of people really seemed to enjoy it it always seemed more for established businesses. Opening up the free tier has given me the chance to try it out for personal projects and some freelance work. As business grows, could definitely see using this on a paid plan because of how intuitive things are. Just very well thought out in the structure of it all.

The company I work for uses Basecamp countless times on a daily basis. On desktop computers. The app...is another story. I have never had full functionality of the app on my phone. 95% of the time no campfire conversations, pings, to-do's etc etc ever loaded, regardless of how long I waited. The other 5% of the time it functioned after restarting the app multiple times. Since the most recent couple of updates, it now doesn't load 100% of the time. This kind of app is not viable for start-ups at!

All that is needed is to be able to easily create a job (task) assign it to an employee, and set a due date.. It shouldn't be that hard.. It seems like the most basic of operations. But none of these PM apps have that ability.. Just because an app is robust, doesn't mean that it has to be impossible.. I'm familiar with SAP, and this app shouldn't be that difficult to navigate. Why not make it simple?
Great productivity tool with a ton of great features but most importantly it's one of the easiest programs for you and your team to learn and adapt to. Highly recommend it

Basecamp is better than any other productivity and team co-ordinating app I've ever used. I first discovered it about 10 years ago when I was working in a small team in an indie business. Recently started using it again to coordinate our small events company, it's been invaluable. It's a shame they don't do a free personal account anymore, I guess that speaks to their success. From a freelancers POV though it'd be awesome if they brought that option back though.

You can do everything in the app that you can do on the website, which is great. The chat however is quite slow, often messages don't appear for some time even though the system notification arrives. Very often you will see a "disconnected, waiting for connection.." on the chat side, which is quite frustrating. So the app is good to catch up on work while on the road, but for heavy interaction with colleagues it's unfortunately sub-par to other solutions.

Basecamp is easily the most impressive work productivity platform I've come across. It combines the important aspects of many other work products into one, and I strongly suspect that Basecamp will result in less time wasted and more time spent getting things done. My only criticism is that there should be a cheaper paid account. Small companies shouldn't be paying the same as big companies. Why not a $20 option for increasing the project count to 10 or 20? Basecamp throwing away money imo...

App: generally works pretty well, but has a few issues that are sometimes quite problematic. I haven't found a way to copy links from people's posts, for when you need to open them in a different browser. Sometimes I open a notification in the app, and out never loads, but still makes it as read. Basecamp is pretty good mostly, but it's really let down by having an atrocious search function, making it really hard to find things, or check if someone's already said what you want to say.

The Android version feels less polished than the iOS version. For example, the toolbar at the bottom only displays on a few screens meaning that as you start diving into projects it becomes cumbersome to get back to the homepage - this doesn't happen on iOS. There's also no widgets available, whereas on iOS there's an events calendar.

For the amount the two founders boast about it, it is lacking in features. Credit too; it is reliable, it is well built, serves its purpose, and does come with a less-stress workflow. It's also refreshing there are Devs focusing on LESS. Overall 4/5 because to get a project done and be able to capture everything (with Git/Hub) it works really well.
excellent company as long as they're the same owners , great organization to try free the. eventually upgrade whenever you want no pressure

I'm willing to concede that I might be missing the point here but I find this program to be riddled with redundancy and overall clunky to use. I think it is a poor excuse of a social media platform and if my church would abandon it I would quite gladly just use text message and email.

Works well enough. I like that you can sync events to a Google calendar. Unfortunately, if you choose the wrong email the first time, you can't seem to change it. So make sure you sync correctly THE FIRST TIME. Otherwise this important feature is useless. The Add To Calendar option only appears half the time anyway.

We are using Basecamp 3 for our group of Lay/Care Pastors, and Pastors in our Church. In the beginning it's a learning curve. I had to adjust the notifications. I don't want email notifications and App notifications. Finding the notification settings could be easier. I chose to only receive notifications in my phones home screen whenever I "don't have the App open."

In latest update, it said "Improved ping experience". That's not what I'm facing. Can anyone tell me how I can find a group ping where I don't know all constituent members and if it was done 12 different pings before with no new notification from that ping? Instead of improved, say degraded Ping experience by removing the whole section.

Nice collaboration and teaching tool with rich functionality, but too complex. And the kichy wording for categories makes it even more challenging to navigate. I'm taking a course using basecamp and am finding the tool getting in the way. It's causing information and navigation overload. I suggest you run focus groups of users of multiple types. Also, impossible to put newlines in posts. If it's possible, it's certainly not obvious.

When I install it I got a message saying something like "purchase complete". I signed up for the free personal version on my PC. I don't want to "purchase" anything! Uninstalling now. PS. I went back and tried installing again in case I was missing something. This time there was no mention of purchase. I hope that doesn't mean I am already paying for it, because I only want the free version to manage a few DIY jobs on my house. I will review my rating again once I have used the app.

I rate it 4 stars only because I have constant problems loading images to each project. If the images could be saved and slowly downloading over time instead of deleting itself. I could then continue the download after I resume cell phone service. All in all it is a fantastic app. I just wish the uploading of photos becomes easier. I am an android user if that helps.

If you've downloaded Basecamp, then most-likely you know what you're looking for. Basecamp is a bad-ass Project Management tool that can help you make plans and execute them. They're methodology is A-typical, but once you understand that, then it's smooth sailing from there. I very rarely give 5-stars because I'm a perfectionist and there's always room for improvement, but it's close! Enjoy.

No Scrolling In Docs & Files. We use this app at my job. I have been inquiring about a scrolling feature to move back and forth through files/photos for YEARS. Much like a gallery function when you have a photo or doc open. You have to exit out + hunt for the spot you were in to then look at the next or previous photo/file that was near/next to the one you were just looking at. This is a HUGE PIA when you upload a ton of photos which we tend to do to create other content like before+after pix.

Coming from Slack I've always found this app hard to use. I don't know if it's just me or the app design but I have a hard time finding things. I use it for communication with a collaborative group and don't have a choice. Slack makes more sense to me. Yellow is a very harsh color, not crazy about the new icon. Clashes with my wallpaper.

I enjoy base camp but I cannot rate this app hi until they fix the glitch of "chat is offline please try again later" that constantly plagues me. It's pretty much every single time I go to respond to anything my coworkers have sent me on base camp. I have the most updated version of the app and my phone is also up to date. It's incredibly annoying to not be able to send an immediate response and have to close and open the app 5 to 7 times in order to get a simple message sent through.

Edit: changes aren't improvements Pretty good. Older version was even better when you could applaud messages of team members with one click. The boosts are a hassle and I don't use them as a result. Especially on mobile having to type something instead of a simple click. Kinda feel spoiled though. Still pretty good. But take something amazing away for something mediocre, feels like I'm missing a great functionality.

Amazing platform for tracking progress, holding employees, team members accountable as well as keeping clients updated. Only issue I have is sometimes you have to close app and restart to view photos/updates. I suppose that's a small price to pay for all of the other good things it does only reason why I would give it 4 stars out of 5. Otherwise I would highly recommend this app for any business with a lot of moving parts.

Two stars due to a mix of the general structure of Basecamp, and the sheer amount of use it gets by my fire department. The option to either get notified about EVERYTHING or only when you're tagged/pinged is a bit too broad. My agency adds documents and training info and other notifications multiple times daily which basically creates a ton of noise with no way to actually filter out what I need to know vs. info not relevant to me.

The app just doesn't work. Anytime I try to load it the projects will not load. I have tried multiple times to reload, uninstall and reinstall, same problem and it does not matter if I am on or off wifi. Same problem. The app is useless which is unfortunate. Hopefully it is a bug that can be fixed. My phone is a Samsung S8.

Outstanding team communication platform! Great app too. I have been using basecamp for at least 12 years. It's the only platform I have used that works like people's brains. Easy to learn and teach others to use! App is extremely functional and allows me to manage a wide range of teams from anywhere. I am a big fan!

I cannot say enough amazing things about Basecamp. As a creative director I always had issues managing files and deadlines, and while it's my job to steer the ship sometimes I found myself dropping the ball because of organization. I love how visual it is and how the various features like a shared calendar, direct message pings and the live timeline feature helps put my mind at ease knowing that if I'm not there, the work is still being complete.

I think it is fantastic for project management but the notifications need to be fixed. It would be better to have an option to get a notification each time one of my staff talks in the campfire. I mean we could make this an optional one but it's like having absolutely no notifications on your WhatsApp unless someone @mentions your name first which is damn weird. I have to rely on pings for DMs on important issues and even then, the notifications on note 9 and computer is buggy as hell.

On mobile: People tags will often disappear shortly after adding them to a post. If you're quick enough you can hit "Post" and they'll stay, but if not, they will disappear and the only way to add them is for the to edit the post and add the people tags again.

A book store with alot of part time booksellers needs a path of continuity day-to-day to keep staff informed and customer service at peak. Basecamp is that staff person who is literally there every day and has helped stop irritating paper memos posted on the wall! Campfire messages, pings, shared documents all add to our efficiency and customer record keeping. It will receive 5 stars from me when the app opens reliably for me at my home. I often can navigate but not access content.

Basecamp is perfect; the mobile apps are anything but. Should you use Basecamp for project management? Absolutely, yes. We run an architecture firm and use it to manage all our projects, and it's incredibly powerful yet easy to adopt. The mobile apps are quite usable too, but they don't support any background processing at all and are extremely slow to download and upload anything. Unlike WhatsApp, you have to sit and wait forever for small 1 MB files to download/upload. Otherwise, it's fine.

I have to work with this app because of my contractors but this is the buggiest app ever. The app shows no network connections where other apps get it fine and almost always when I open any screen I just see grey bars, I have to back up multiple times to get it to work. And it's not a device thing as I have used in 3 different ones. Edit: I was using chrome canary UIvwebview client, once changing back to chrome the issue was resolved. Did not know this was a hybrid app.

The new UI update is not good for users like myself with lots of projects. The compact list view is gone, as is the option to view archived projects. All that's left are these giant cards that only display a few characters of the title. It's not useful at all. Even on the desktop version they took away the option to make list view the default. I'm getting tired of these "one size fits all" design choices that clearly don't work for everyone. Give me a toggle so I can go back to using the app like it was before.

I recently installed this application and attempted to create a new project. Upon copying text into the description field, I encountered an issue – the application failed to save the text in the project description. This seemingly straightforward action, the saving of a project description, inexplicably did not work, and no error messages were displayed. How can I justify paying $350 per month for such a service? 🤯

I would very much like to use this app. But I am having the same problem as the other recent post. Nothing is loading, not even slowly. The app loads fine. The content is absent. Such a great service that I would consider paying for, but I can't use this until the app issues are worked out. This company has a long track record of getting it right. Unfortunately not here. But hopefully their commitment to the user experience will kick in and it will get fixed soon. Android 8.1.0, Moto g5 Plus.

This app is amazing for our volunteer organization to keep track of updates, uploading pictures and keeping in contact. I especially love the seemless switch when using it on my phone or laptop. I can start a draft at either location and finish it on another. It's so handy and user friendly.

Seriously buggy. I go to write a post and regularly lose blocks of text, parts of sentences, or random words and cannot go back and edit previous paragraphs without breaking the entire post. Private messages are annoying to access at best. Great platform with a terrible app.

It's not my choice of app but the group I am with use it so I have too. It is really difficult to upload documents and put where you want them. Big problem is that it crashes a lot of the time. Like just now I tryed to open a message and it crashed. They need to sort out these glitches.

The most convoluted login process for an app I've seen to date - 5 attempts and I still can't get it to work. Still remembers my old account from a previous job, either gets stuck endlessly loading when I try to change it, or just turns off. The desktop version is nice but this thing is a waste of time.

Great app! However, would be best if you can add a Status in each to-dos, e.g: Pending, In Progress, On Hold, Completed. Not only the tick-box that says the project/task is done and maybe you could also add a nested list for every orderlist (I'm not sure if this makes sense) but what I mean here is like for example; Main Folder >New Folder >New Folder...but in list type I just do the folder as example for what I want to see in the basecamp app. Anyway,I hope to see these in your next updates :)

Been using the app for years at work and it's great. The only issue I have is the the "mark as read" feature doesn't work. Whenever you mark a message as read (swiping) the message keeps coming back up. It's been an issue for months now.

Wrote a whole list out including pictures, over an hour and half and lost everything by accidentally swiping my thumb downwards. Apparently it refreshed and deleted all my information. Trying to include someone or more is a frustrating thing as well. Is says "include all or include no one"...no choices of choosing and if you try to tap on include all it does nothing. Not very user friendly, very frustrating and at the end of a work day having to abandon the app and write every thing down
Truly the only no nonsense project management solution that you need!
Very responsive and really take care with your needs and act accordingly.

the app refuses to show me every project I've been invited to under the line up. it only displays one at a time so each time I have to re click the invite. and I don't see a way to manually find a teacher, class, or project if the invite doesn't work.

For the last seven years, Basecamp has been an excellent way for me and my siblings to coordinate helping my parents in their later years. It is a brainstorming platform, a to-do list, a record keeping system, a contacts reference and a timeline. I feel like it has helped our whole family grow closer by enabling us to communicate in a variety of ways, by texting and sending photos in the Campfire feature, to private one-on-on Pings, to affirming Boosts. Thank you, Basecamp!
It's just easy to find solve problem with issue abject but I don't know superme crout

UPDATE: We continue to times in Basecamp as our core business too for project management. Basecamp continues to take it's product further. We continue to take out subscription further. They are always communications, always owning their bugs, and always making it better. So far, Basecamp has proven to be exactly what we need to take our small business to the next level. It has empowered delegation and planning significantly.

I have tinkered, tested and actually used basecamp since, I think 2004. I knew that it was a special, very different type of app from the start. And for years I didn't know any other basecamp users. I have utilized most of their awesome classic apps for many years. When it comes to remote learning culture, the basecamp people have helped write the book, blaze the path. It has proven to be one of the most reliable, time tested project management apps. I am writing this review because possibly I don't want my population to try an reinvent the wheel. Here's the thing: Just google basecamp, follow Jason Fried videoson youtube and listen a weekly podcast called...Rework. And this fall and winter getting used to using basecamp for your go to project management tool. And as 2021 comes around, you'll be well on your way to grasping how remote / virtual learning actually flows. Have fun!

App struggles to load if not on fast wifi. Have no problems with slack. Haven't been many, or any, improvements to the software in the last few years. So many opportunities to improve the software. Seems lazy at this point.

I run a small marketing agency and Basecamp has been a total game-changer for us! I didn't even realize how much time we wasted every day getting organized, checking on the status of projects, sharing information, etc. It has more than paid for itself. We won't ever be without it!

I see some of the reviews on receiving emails from Basecamp and not being able to handle them. Hey has found to do just that solving this huge issue... You can still see emails in your inbox without feeling overwhelmed by the number of alerts... Use the Bundle function pls.

i have the same issues with regards to being allowed to upload files.... it just stopped working. yes the app has permission for camera and storage. it looks like there was an update and now it doesn't do anything with regards to uploading... New update has the same glitch... cannot upload directly from the camera ... then you added back the small circle plus ➕ and it worked... now you took it away and it still doesn't work ... android and fully updated... Does anyone test before tweaking?

I would give it 4 plus if it wasn't for the fact that the chat is always "off line" on Android. That makes using the app difficult. Everything else works well. The web version is great and our company has been using it for over 6 years.

Unable to sort items on to-do lists by dragging. No option to sort items in a to-do list by creation date, due date, alphabetically. Would like to move to basecamp from Microsoft to-do, but don't think I'll be able to until this is addressed.

They keep changing the UI and continually make it harder to use in the search for a "clean look". I need to be able to see all my projects on the home page, not "the ones I've pinned" Now they have hidden comments on a thread making it even harder to find information

So many bugs. Every update comes with new bugs. On the desktop, Basecamp is fantastic. But the mobile app is not the same. The latest update - can't attach PDFs to the task, can't tag more than 2 people on the task when creating it, very slow loading of images posted on the task and the likes. We use Basecamp for all our co-ordination, and when in the field, this app is our backbone. But unfortunately fails most of the time. Please fix the bugs!

As an inbound marketing agency we've been super impressed with Basecamp. We used to use other task management software but it just doesn't do what basecamp does. If you're just starting out or looking to switch give Basecamp a try.

We test drove other project management apps. None were right for our small real estate development business. We needed a project management app that was lightweight and intuitive so we could get everyone using it with minimal training or friction. Basecamp ticked all the boxes. Creating task lists, communicating, tracking, all the things. And it's a great UX. And the desktop, mobile, and browser versions are all outstanding.

Basecamp keep continue to change the way of working. This application is a reflection of their approach. Everything Smith, clean and easy to use. Thank you guys. By the way, only negative think about the app, it doesn't work offline. After being addicted to this application not using it in the underground makes me sad.

Disappointed ... I'm a freelancer. I have multiple clients on Basecamp. I cant switch between them. I need to be able to move not only between projects but clients. Forcing me to a laptop is just annoying.

It is absolutely 100% awesome, except that frequently, the notifications don't work. I'll log on and find that 12 things happened, and I didn't get notified a single time. Other than that, it's a pretty decent app.

Bookmarks could be better unless I'm not using them properly; and being able to see the opening narrative always, I feel, is important. sometimes I just can't get to see them at all. It's such a simple thing to do surely, yet it's far too complicated and time-consuming.

It's a very simple, fast and lightweight project management app that suits my needs. But, why is "Add a to-do" button is at the end of any list? It makes us slower for the long to-do lists. this change still not implemented for a year or i didn't get a response from the team.

This is a super useful tool for collaboration and getting things done in a team setting. I do find all the different places to converse as slightly confusing. I love the flexibility of all the widgets. Really great tool.
I don't see how it helps organize. I find out most difficult to use as a customer.

So far its been the most consistant platform for schduling jobs and projects with no issues. Today post update we had issues with iphone users not being able to comment on jobs. About a total of 15 mins talking with support and the issue was fixed. Great customer support and reaction time.

Basecamp is a great project managing app for any business. We've been using Basecamp for over 4 years to manage 100+ client accounts, using the "projects" feature for each individual client and the "teams" for managing internal departments' tasks and communications. On top of that, the fact that you can invite clients and control what they see is a great feature for collaborating and ensuring full transparency of our services.

Can't search within a thread. Cannot see all my pings in one list. Have to use the web browser version to see a list of pings but they are all icons, need a list view with last msg showing as a preview. Very qwerky overall across all platforms.

Very bad UX. 1) No progress (% wise) shown for downloads/uploads. 2) Need to upload a pic 4-5 times to send it. Sometimes, even then it doesn't upload. 3) Message loading is very slow. 4) Message sync is slow too. 4) Notifications appear with a lot of delay.

Great app to simply keep track of things between a small group of people, whether for a project or small business. I've used Basecamp off and on for years and it just works, without overcomplicating everything.

Looked interesting but no dark or black mode for people with bad eyesight. In addition, no black mode to avoid battery usage. You have to cater for poor eyesight. 12 months laqter (20Mar22) still no dark or black mode. Is it too difficult? Maybe ask Google how to do it? Won't allow uploading a logo on a Note20 Ultra 5G 512gb phone, or a Samsung galaxy S5e tablet. Tried everything.
Not everything is easy to find or intuitive, but the overall function of the platform is versatile and I have learned to use it well.

I like the app, but sometimes, the notifications are a bit much. My employees have started to ignore any notifications. I wish I could set this to just indie them when something new is sent their way.

Transitioning from leader/engineer/geek mode to team mode is a deliberative process. This has been and continues to be a great asset and tool for bridging the communication gap. We experience better and consistent positive consequences toward achieving profitability and flow as a result of the overall ease of adoption. The team functions more as a body is a collection of parts. That's a definite win in our book!

A lot of helpful improvements. But now the "catch up" is missing. Was super handy to view large amount of notifications with this card style swipe . Now navigation experience in the notifications is not as fast and clear as it is used to be

The only "flow" this creates is a flow of colorful words while I try to quickly navigate something so simplistic that it appears that it would allow you to work quickly however you must open a tab to open a section tonupload or view files. My web team uses this and I am failing to understand why...waste of money and resources on the web if you ask me. I already have a project management tool that does all this does and looks more business than this playful, gen z appeasing app/website.

Terrible platform. Basic features you would expect like a project calendar to be synced to your accounts calendar are missing. Also, you don't get notifactions for calendar entries and exports to google calendar don't work correctly.

I have to delete and redownload this app every single time I want to check my work new schedule because when I try to get to the second screen everything is blank. I try restarting the app multiple times and my phone and nothing works. Honeslty so frustrated with this app.Hoping this update fixes these problems so I can change this comment.

On a computer this website is great and works. This app however is another story. I've list count how many times I've uninstalled and installed this app just so that I can check the message boards of different projects or to send a ping.

Best method for arranging all my different projects, has made my life simpler. Basically, before this app, my work was chaotic, afterwards there isn't a day I stress because I know everything is in order and going to plan. Helps that I'm a pothead, this app is my memory ;)

Great on onboarding experience, everything I was looking for in a planning app. They give really good guidance on how to use each feature and I love that you can attach a message/note/comment to almost anything! Makes it easy to organize my thoughts in relation to each Todo. Highly recommend!

A good app on phone, but riddled with a major bug on Chrome OS (maybe tablet) where frequently, but not always, all the displayed text spreads out and only displays some of the characters in words making it unsuable most of the time...
Great way to communicate with clients, collaborators, students. Maintaining project deadlines, putting all files in one place, monitoring progress of milestones was never easier for me before.

Deleted.....I can't get past the login in page. It asks to delete all my other work files. It's a device permission issue (I think). Such a pity as I need to have access when away from my desktop. I had no luck with Basecamp 2 either.
Awesomeness! Does work on browser, mac and android perfectly well. Sometimes switching between groups and pings becomes slow after using for a while. On mac app this happens more often than android.
Although it's a good collaboration tool, it's not my favorite collaboration tool. Sometimes it's hard to tell what the newest post in a thread is, which has caused alarm over resolved issues.

I would give zero stars but it is not possible. My user account had an issue with a social login provider and it is now useless. Everything is lost. Months of work, comments, and assignments are lost forever. Basecamp is unable to support and fix. Stay away from Basecamp. Terrible user experience.

Very useful! Great for team communication and efficiency. Excellent support from the company. Expensive for small budgets for the unlimited version, but an excellent product and worth it if your budget is large enough.

Couldn't do without. Professional solution to connect your team, staff, or project group with lots of helpful features for every type of organization. After testing a few apps like this, we decided this was the way to go.

Has been great for keeping track of our equipment and streamlining the booking of equipment, trucks, labor and specialty tools. I can easily track down what I need within the company by simply putting through a message using this app rather than texting individuals that can lead to a goose chase

I've been following Basecamp ever since they were 37 Signals and using Basecamp on and off. I have been using Basecamp for Android since last 6 months for my regular project management assignments. There are times when you find Basecamp gets handy and you get the work done
First impression: the app is missing some basic testing. For example, one major problem I encountered within the first few minutes, the avatar is not updated after uploading a new photo.
We are quite a small org, and the easy interface is very much appreciated. Easy access means efficiency and clarity. A really good app.

Fantastic program (I'm new to it now but it sure seems devoted and sincere!) that I'm working through with a group from a Church that I believe everyone should give a try. At the very least, you should gain some spiritual insight into your troubles that may be bothering you in your mind...
For what my company is using it for, Its not good. Many other tools that we should be using. So much stuff everywhere. Its complicated and a mess.

Great app for us service engineers, great way to keep in touch with the office without having to rely on email, calls etc. Creating a unique topic for each customer to share all info, emails, meeting minutes, drawings etc. Brilliant tool to have.
I rarely get notifications. Reply to Basecamp. I pretty much only use Basecamp on my phone. My laptop is off 99% of the time. I get random notifications but never is it close to all of them.
As a contractor this app has made it so much easier to keep everyone in the loop, having multiple jobs going at once can be stressful but having the to-do list helps keeping everything on track.

Garbarge. Continually indicates "Oops, we're having trouble loading that page" Keep hitting Refresh but nothing. If it's because I'm trialling it and all would be hunky dory if I paid, then that makes it even worse
It's such a badly 5 app. I got logged out and couldn't figure out to log back in. It kept asking me to sign the company? What!

Probably one of the best project management tools out there! Really delivers ok combining a bunch of other tools all in one place. Everything is bundled in a nice "Project" folder. Never have to ask my designer where the design assets are again.
Lightweight and useful, working for its purpose. but why not putting the "add a to do" button at the top of the lists also?
I love Basecamp! It keeps me and my team organized with our communication, projects, and tasks. There's a bit of a learning curve, but it is well worth the time invested to learn.
The app is fantastic. Although I love the older version where you can check and respond to all pings. This updated android version takes away the ping icon. Can the ping icon be come back please?

Our team has been utilizing this app for a few years and its so convenient to add and delete people and assign teams to projects. Its helpful when munlti organizations are working together on a project in the field (rural Alaska) and have access to notes to submit or share google docs and spreadsheets and to upload photos. We are enjoying the app.

Great app but has two issues. Firstly struggles to load if there isn't near perfect wifi or data connextion. Secondly have just changed the icon to some awful yellow concoction that hurts my eyes everytime I open it or my phone 🤮🤮
When sending pings, I regularly get the error "Chat is offline, try again in a bit." Then I have to login on the computer, which defeats the purpose of having an app.
I suggest improving the media upload so that I can share multiple image files at once. And, eliminating the delay in mobile notification so that I can keep up with my team's office flow.
Pretty great for doing most tasks. I wish comments would have a draft for mobile since they take up full screen, so you can't reference what you're replying to without losing what you've typed.
Good app When do you expect to add dark mode like desktop version? I'm really enjoying the dark mode in desktop, now it's very easy to read with attention. The strain in the eyes is now much less.

The power of this app comes from: 1. how you choose the tools to use 2. how often do you enter useful information 3. how do you link information. The best app I have played with, to solve problems using the power I mentioned above.

we've been using base camp for about 2 or 3 years for our small family office and it's awesome. the app is definitely a working project but I don't mind growing with it. the browser version is a chefs kiss kinda perfect!
The "improved" pings is much worse. Change it back! I don't want to go to "hey" for messages; messages tend to be more important whereas there's a lot of junk extra notifications.

Not sure of a new spiffy logo helps with user experience. However so far the app has been great in improving the productivity of me and company I work with. Don't put tasks and reminders into your head, delegate them to Basecamp. The project based message boards is a breath of fresh air from the usual chat-like team productivity tool.

Hate the alarming yellow construction hat. Loved the green smiling hill. I will be removing this app from my homescreen and hiding it in a folder because that yellow is so ugly and uninviting, which means I will be engaging much less frequently. I would change my rating much higher if you change the visuals back. Who ever advised you to adopt the yellow hasn't done you any favors.
an excellent way of organising team work. like all tools, it needs to be tailored to the task and people involved. Basecamp is flexible enough without being overwhelming to be tailored.

They have a team of designers working on your logo. but not one of them gave me any professional opinions for my son logo. I sent them pictures and gave great details but they were still unable to preform. I am asking for my money back and they are refusing to do so. I will post the very unsatisfied logos that they did if I don't get my money back. I tried to back out of deal in the beginning because the first designer was very disrespectful and they pleaded with me to give them another chance

excessive limitations if you don't buy premium. can only create 1 or 2 tasks before its unusable. also, no option in the settings to remove your acct/opt out of constant emails after Uninstalling the app.

What a fantastic app. As students attempting to make their club a success, we encountered various difficulties in managing and planning our events. Basecamp's great features and soliciting solved those issues. 5 out of 5 stars.

I am quite having a hard time accessing the other areas of basecamp. It works perfectly using ping but not with accessing docs&files, campfire, and such. I am currently using an LG G4 Stylus phone. Hope my concern will be addressed. Thank you.
Great app in communicating
Navigation isn't easy. Getting blasted with every message is annoying. Messaging should be more direct to specific departments without auto blasting an entire staff.
it's okay. allows our group to communicate and save documents in one spot to make it easier to find. not super intuitive for me.
I like Basecamp and would give it a five if they ever get the chat fixed. Its always offline and you have to refresh 3 or 4 times to send a message....very frustrating.

It has become very easy to manage task and conversations in one place with Basecamp. It may seem a small benefit however, as the number of client and projects grow, it becomes chaotic very quickly. With Basecamp, everything is easy and no task is unfinished and goes unnoticed which improves TAT and subsequently client satisfaction.
It's a confusing and slow tool. The company i consult for uses it extensively. Imo, Basecamp has negatively impacted productivity and created a communication gap across the entire company.

Amazing tool for organizing teams and keeping track of things. The team is super responsive too. In some cases Jason fried himself replied to my YouTube comment. Gotta say I'm very happy with the product so far.
Awesome for labs and study groups. Great organization of different types of notifications and the support help is very friendly and helpful. No complaints, great app and great site.
One of the worst collaboration apps out there. Difficult to use, not intuitive and generally hard work. I would say that the developers have achieved the exact opposite of what they intended.
Won't let me add things half the time and it's super annoying. Limits productivity when I have to try to do the same thing 5 to 6 times

New users to Basecamp get flooded with emails. Admins have no option to fix this and change notifications for their users. This has caused us a great deal of frustration for our users, training headaches for admins and losing volunteers before they get started. Before Basecamp rolls out any new features, they need to fix this obnoxious problem yesterday.
Very hard to navigate and update. Also inconsistant in its notifications. If this was not needed for work I would not use it.
Excellent way to communicate with clients. A little confusing at times but the search function makes up for that.
Fantastic small team and large team project management tool. Works, intuitive, and keeps our high school robotics team on track! We LOVE Basecamp!
Love it. Always gets better, and it can support any type of project I could imagine. I seem to be able to do everything on the mobile app that I can do on desktop.
Best app I have ever found! Been using it for my business for the last 3 years. The best part is they listen to their users they tweaked the app with our suggestions. Highly Recommend!
Terrible app. Sluggish and gives the equivalent of spinning wheels a lot of the time. Better to access basecamp directly from browser.
Confusing and cluttered layout makes it difficult to navigate. If I wasn't forced to use it for work, I would uninstall and never look back.
doesn't load . doesn't show all my notifications. I've had to remove the ap and re download the ap numerous times this week alone. if I didn't need it for work then I wouldn't have it at all

Best task management, project management, team work management app I've ever used. It is free for 3 projects and up to 20 users now. Just try it, you'll fall in love with Basecamp. I appreciate them for making such great app for free.
I've used BaseCamp for several years with the company I work for, and we love it. Having worked with many project management systems, BaseCamp is far and beyond the my favorite of them.
Why the hell it's with this yellow icon... It was nice with green and I was fine with it... Now yes strange. About the app and the whole platform - just perfect!!! You're the best out there.

I have this since July and they still not fix this issue. Everytime I chat once and chat another for second time. It says "Chat is offline. Try again a little bit". I always encounter this problem. I need to quit the app before I can chat again.
I love Basecamp on PC, but on my S9+ the app just never loads my tasks, schedules, anything beyond the home page. The app is basically worthless which is a shame.
This new icon is so depressing. 😭 The old one looked way better! It was happy and made me feel all warm & fuzzy inside. Please change the icon back!
The app is constantly freezing when I'm trying to type out my EOD report. Not very useful when the app isn't working properly.
Unfortunately very unreliable. Chats rarely work, even when connected to wifi. Extremely frustrating.
This app is absolutely amazing! Web and Mobile, this is truly exactly what I need. I recommend this product a lot for people who like to organise their things and work. Thanks
Great app to connect teams and get the job done. Only issue is how slow the app is and how long it takes to upload stuff.
Basecamp is a fantastic app. I've been using it for a few years now within my organization and it does a great job organizing our projects and team.
Basecamp mobile app is always a pleasure to use. I never feel like I'm using a crippled version of them website. Fully functional and rarely any issues.

This software has greatly improved communication and overall performance of my multi-location franchise business. Basecamp allows me to work on my business while not needing to be physically in my business.
I like it, but is not user intuitive. It took a while to figure out where everything was. I would usually bookmark everything so I'd be able to find it later.
I'm a big fan of Basecamp but hated the android app due to poor performance on large threads. This current release has fixed those issues. 5 stars from me now. Good work guys.
Slow or no loading of information when I open the app. Also, not receiving notifications on my phone. Only through emails.
Please, add sound pop-up for pings and other notification. This app can be perfect if you add sound.
I've been using Basecamp daily for about twenty years and I'm still loving it. It's a philosophy of practical productivity without the stress.
Wonderful app! I appreciate the design. It's quick to get started and also to pop in and add something or check a task off. Thanks!!
If you find it difficult to work in a organised manner then Basecamp is your life saver. Especially I love their doc sharing and auto check-in features.
Amazing task tracker. The best I've used so far. The mobile app is great, it's ok to use it and have some work done even when you outside

Has delay issues with notification, page loading, chat, etc. Recommended basecamp to my company and many colleagues told app should work more smoothly. Hoping to see a better optimized update very soon.
If a project/team has a bunch of activity your phone doesn't constantly blow up like a group chat. Love it. This has been amazing for our growing robotics team.
Good concept, but it's easy to lose track of what you want to work on, and challenging just to retrieve it quickly when you need it.
Been using Basecamp for a few days, and I think it is the best collaborative organizational platform I have ever come across. Plan to ask my bosses to buy the paid version!!
Very heavy app.. Takes a lot of time to open.. But very secure and user friendly. Makes things very easy
The "mark all as read" option has disappeared making the 'hey' function totally unbearable. Please add it back!
Most excellent tool. Basecamp staff is very responsive, even for the free version.
Been using the app for about 3 months now and operationally I can't fault it... there are a few more features I would like to see in future development.
Incredible experience! Easy to use and get started right away. I'm a big Basecamp fan from Basecamp 1 and when the ran a suite of apps.
This app is awesome. Very convenient. But I need a Dark Mode like in the desktop version. Hope you make this update soon.
In the latest update, everything I try to comment on a task, it auto refreshes after a few seconds and I lose the whole message.
Love this app. Great for my business and writing and reading team daily check-ins. Fast, slick and a pleasure to use. Well done Android team!!!

been usin app for a month now.... its not user friendly.. you need to open a dif page just to go to a room of pages... make it simple.. just 1 page.. contains all chatroom... i would choose not to use app if i am given a choice... just sharing..
Im surprised that in 2022, this app still does not allow downloading an entire folder or multiple files. I have to download each file individually.
love the product. I love that it syncs with calendars. I just wish that there was some compatibility to adjust a calendar event made on basecamp...on Google calendar.

The app is slow, pings and campfire slow to load and laggy. Adding images, files etc feels clunky. Can't use Basecamp on my Chromebook so now must rely entire on phone app and experience is not as good.

User friendly!!! However, its best to add an individual notification on each project or team when you go to home for you to see in one glance which project or team has an activity since its realy difficult to scan all activity in one general notification. Hope this will be added soon 👍
Cannot tag people using the phone app. The tag is put into the message, but disappears within seconds. I can provide a screen recording if you contact me.
No dark mode, I would rather use the website version for this. The UI is not good either. The app team should contact the web team as soon as possible. I really want a good app for Basecamp.
Not user friendly. To-dos keep coming back after you've acknowledged they are complete, and can't figure out how to upload documents.
does not receive ping messages, the option is not even found in the app. The icon tells you you have unread messages but you can't find the pings unless you use a laptop
I have this app on my chromebook and it keeps saying to update it, but then it closes before I can do it and I don't see the tab that allows me to in the playstore.
I'm having trouble uploading docs and files. I wound appear after so many attempts also tried restarting but still it just wouldn't upload files and attach files in posts/message.
This is an excellent tool. The app works well. Even when you disable notifications. I'm sure they'll continue to develop. I've used Basecamp for 8 years.
This app is consistently, inconsistent. what is the point if the chat is offline more often than not? When it works, it is great but I have issues with this app every single day, guaranteed

...we are scheduling and communicating tasks, to-dos, and follow-ups for three church locations. We also use it for small team tasks within each location. A well designed and easy-to-use solution in today's busy world!

No question the best project management software I've ever used. It literally is everything you need in one area. I've read their books and have been a customer for years now. BC3 is such a great improvement! Keep it up 👍

the best for of organization and communication, the creates of Basecamp rocked it! and it's the only system I feel comfortable with the upgrades because I know that each step is taking in the whole process in consideration. thank you base camp for keeping us connected and organized!
Very good communication tool but messages (Hey!) don't load. I've tried reinstalling a couple of times. Activities work fine. Happy to help track it down.

besides some push notification issues and very very infrequent downtime, this is the most excellent organizational app. and as a dev in a fast scaling company i can understand the glitch here and a bug there, but their response time in fixing issues is immensely fast and their company culture and work ethic is brilliant.
I have to uninstall and re install weekly. I have been using this app for 3 years and for the last 6 months i have this problem every week. Very frustrating
Version 2 was great but Basecamp 3 is way efficient than its predecessor. Yeah billing plan rocks. Highly impressed. I am able to manage projects in a very simpler way.
Simple App that works to communicate and collect various items of a project with a team. Could improve scheduling to include an address field but the notes section will work for now. 😁
